zh.lifeSummary: 當Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare於1094年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford29歲，母親Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont30歲。她於1108年之前與Aubrey II de Vere在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她於1145年在英國艾可被列為corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich。她去世於1163年11月1日在英國艾可聖 奧斯享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可聖 奧斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare于1094年出生在英国賀福爾时，她的父亲Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford29岁，母亲Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont30岁。她于1108年之前与Aubrey II de Vere在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她于1145年在英国艾可被列为corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich。她去世于1163年11月1日在英国艾可聖 奧斯享年69岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可聖 奧斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Adeliza
meanings: Corrupted from Adelaide

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare was born in 1094, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, was 29 and her mother, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, was 30. She married Aubrey II de Vere before 1108, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. In 1145, her occupation is listed as corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich in Essex, England. She died on 1 November 1163, in St Osyth, Essex, England, at the age of 69, and was buried in St Osyth, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare est née en 1094, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Aubrey II de Vere avant 1108, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. En 1145, la profession de sa indiquée est corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich à Essex, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 1 novembre 1163, à St Osyth, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à St Osyth, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare 1094 in Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford 29 und ihre Mutter Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont 30. Sie hat vor 1108 in Suffolk, England Aubrey II de Vere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1145 ist ihr Beruf als corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich in Essex, England aufgeführt. Sie starb am 1. November 1163 im Alter von 69 Jahren in St Osyth, Essex, England und wurde in St Osyth, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare è nata il nel 1094 nel Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Aubrey II de Vere prima del 1108, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1145, la sua occupazione è indicata come corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich nel Essex, England. Morì il 1 novembre 1163, nel St Osyth, Essex, England, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a St Osyth, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clareが1094年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertfordは29歳，母Adelize en Beauvais de Clermontは30歳であった。1108年以前にイギリススフォルクでAubrey II de Vereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1145年，イギリスエセックスでの職業はcorrodian at st. osyth's priory, chichと記録されている。彼女は1163年11月1日にイギリスエセックススト オシスで69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックススト オシスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare(이)가 1094년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1108년 이전 영국 수폴크에서 Aubrey II de Vere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1145년, 그녀의 직업은 영국 에세스에서 corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich이었다. 그녀는 1163년 11월 1일 영국 에세스 스트 오시스에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 스트 오시스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare nasceu em 1094, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Aubrey II de Vere antes 1108, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1145, sua profissão aparece como corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich em Essex, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu em 1 de novembro de 1163, em St Osyth, Essex, Inglaterra, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em St Osyth, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare родилась в 1094 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Aubrey II de Vere перед 1108 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. В 1145 году она работала по профессии corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich в местности эссекс, Англия. Она умерла 1 ноября 1163 г. в местности ст осит, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности ст осит, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.St. Osyth Priory, St. Osyth, Tendring, E: St Osyth, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare nació en 1094, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Aubrey II de Vere antes de 1108, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1145, su ocupación registrada era corrodian at st. osyth's priory, chich en Essex, Inglaterra. Murió el 1 de noviembre de 1163, en St Osyth, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en St Osyth, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
